rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Office Products Spawning WMI

rules.rule_text:
title: Office Products Spawning WMI
id: 0c2d39af-2c24-42c4-9bab-35e30ad2aeb8
status: experimental
description: "It\u2019s almost always malicious when wmic.exe spawns as a child process\
  \ of Microsoft Office and \nsimilar products. As such, it makes sense to examine\
  \ the chain of execution and follow-on \nactivity when this occurs. The following\
  \ is a non-exhaustive example analytic that will catch \nsome of this activity.\
  \ Part of the RedCanary 2024 Threat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-management-instrumentation/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1047
- attack.t1204
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
    - \winword.exe
    - \excel.exe
    Image|endswith: \wmic.exe
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
